beats.narration_text: Let's recap with Claude. The project builds an AI assistant to interpret Raman spectroscopy of wastewater. It tests on actual wastewater from operational plants, not clean water spiked at high concentrations. Results report the full distribution: best-case, worst-case, and typical performance. Wastewater carries population-level health information decodable through molecular analysis.

artifactLines: The project builds an AI assistant to interpret Raman spectroscopy of wastewater.
It tests on actual wastewater from operational plants, not clean water spiked at high concentrations.
Results report the full distribution: best-case, worst-case, and typical performance.
Wastewater carries population-level health information decodable through molecular analysis.

beats.narration_text: The choice to test the spectroscopy AI on actual wastewater from operational treatment plants, not clean water spiked at inflated concentrations, and to report the full distribution rather than just an average, is what makes this credible to me. Help me: (1) explain why spiked clean-water samples would overstate real-world accuracy; (2) describe what best-case, worst-case, and typical Raman conditions would look like; (3) name what population-level health signal wastewater molecular analysis could decode.
